overview: This volume is entirely dedicated to the Sanad (chains of transmission) of the Hadith of Ghadir Khumm. The author compiles a massive, exhaustive list of prominent Sunni scholars, hadith masters, historians, and exegetes who have narrated, authenticated, and relied upon the hadith. The primary goal is to prove the absolute Tawatur (mass-transmission) of the hadith, refuting any Sunni polemical attempts (such as those by Fakhr al-Razi) to downgrade it to an isolated report (Khabar Wahid) in order to evade its binding theological implications for Imamate.

responses_to_dehlavi.dehlavi_claim_addressed: Hadith of Ghadir Khumm: Dehlavi accepts the hadith as authentic but completely rejects the Shia linguistic and contextual interpretation.
responses_to_dehlavi.author_response_summary: While Dehlavi nominally accepts the hadith's authenticity, the broader Sunni polemical tradition (which Dehlavi builds upon) often attempts to downgrade its epistemological weight by classifying it as an isolated report (Ahad), as explicitly stated by Fakhr al-Razi. The author responds by establishing the undeniable Tawatur (mass-transmission) of the hadith. By proving that it was narrated by over a hundred companions and recorded by dozens of the highest Sunni authorities across all centuries, the author forces the opponent to accept it as Qat'i al-Sudur (absolutely certain in its issuance), which is a prerequisite for establishing foundational theological doctrines like Imamate.
key_evidence_cited: Fakhr al-Razi's admission in 'Mafatih al-Ghayb' and 'Nihayat al-Uqul'
Al-Dhahabi's dedicated work compiling the routes of Ghadir and his authentications in 'Tadhkirat al-Huffaz'
Al-Jazari's explicit declaration of its Tawatur in 'Asna al-Matalib'
Shah Waliullah Dehlavi's narration of it in 'Qurrat al-Aynayn' and 'Izalat al-Khafa'

biographical_dossiers.evaluation_summary: Highly praised as the Imam of his time in theology and exegesis, proving that his admission of the hadith's narration carries immense weight.
Described as a definitive Hujjah (proof), Hafiz, and the Sheikh of the Sunnah in the Levant, validating his inclusion of the hadith in his 'Al-Ahadith al-Mukhtara'.

rijal_sources_cited: Tabaqat al-Shafi'iyya by Ibn Qadi Shuhba
biographical_dossiers.evaluation_summary: Revered as Sheikh al-Islam and a master of hadith and jurisprudence, validating his authentication of the hadith in 'Tahdhib al-Asma wa al-Lughat'.
Acknowledged as the Sheikh of the Hijaz and a great scholar of traditions, validating his extensive narrations of Ghadir in 'Al-Riyad al-Nadira' and 'Dhakha'ir al-Uqba'.

argument_lineage_traced.claim: The Hadith of Ghadir is a Khabar Wahid (isolated report) and thus cannot establish foundational doctrines.
chain: Fakhr al-Din al-Razi (d. 606 AH) in 'Arba'in fi Usul al-Din' -> Later Sunni polemicists

rebuttal_lineage_cited.work: Tadhkirat al-Huffaz / Dedicated treatise on Ghadir
rebuttal_lineage_cited.what_they_refuted: Refuted the claim that the hadith is weak or isolated by compiling its numerous authentic routes.

rebuttal_lineage_cited.work: Asna al-Matalib fi Manaqib Ali b. Abi Talib
rebuttal_lineage_cited.what_they_refuted: Explicitly declared the hadith to be Mutawatir from Ali and from the Prophet, dismissing those who attempt to weaken it as ignorant.
Explicitly classified the Hadith of Ghadir as Mutawatir.
